feedback.text: I am in favour of a regulation on the permissibility of AI, and I always see companies making the worst promises on a daily basis and at the same time veleting copyright. AI may not be used as substitutes for artists and training for AI is contrary to data protection, copyright, false advertising promises. I would also like to say that I find the practice of making video games unplayable for wrong. Often publisher games simply leave the stores (Steam, Epic,etc) more effectively for a long time, and once the servers have been switched off within a few years, I call for the game to disappear properly, even if the server has been switched off or there are no permament online checks. The games should also always be complete on media or the game should be downloaded in full, not by an installer who downloads the game again. And buying in-game should be clearly labelled and not in the necessary element to continue playing. Hopefully, this feedback will help to better regulate AI and video games. M. Stoll from Germany
